description: Safety requirements by task type for electrical construction

ppe: Hearing protection if pulling machine exceeds 85 dB
jsa_items: Verify pulling tension won't exceed cable max sidewall bearing pressure
Check conduit for obstructions/burrs before pull
Verify correct cable staged (check tag vs pull ticket)
Adequate manpower at each pull point
Pulling lubricant compatible with cable jacket
hazards: Pinch points at conduit entries and sheaves
permits: Hot work permit if near hot work areas
Confined space permit if pulling into vaults/manholes
Energized work permit if adjacent circuits live
Fall protection plan if at elevation

overhead_safety: Never use conduit as support for climbing
underground_hazards: Trench collapse — shore or slope per OSHA
Striking existing utilities — verify dig permit
hazardous_area_rules: RMC or MI cable required in Class I Div 1 — EMT NOT permitted
Sealing fittings within 18 in of enclosure in Div 1
Seals at boundary from classified to unclassified
5 full threads minimum on RMC in hazardous locations

loto: Follow site LOTO procedure — zero energy verification required
Each worker applies own lock and tag
Verify zero energy with rated voltage tester
Test tester on known live source before and after
Stored energy: discharge capacitors, release springs

arc_flash_categories.cat1_4to8_cal: Arc-rated shirt/pants min 4 cal, face shield, hard hat, leather gloves
arc_flash_categories.cat2_8to25_cal: Arc-rated clothing min 8 cal, flash hood or face shield/balaclava
arc_flash_categories.cat3_25to40_cal: Arc-rated flash suit min 25 cal, flash hood, rubber insulating gloves
arc_flash_categories.cat4_40_cal: Arc-rated flash suit min 40 cal, full flash suit hood

best_practices: Verify conductor size matches lug rating
Correct crimp die for compression lugs
Torque all connections to manufacturer spec

safety: Test voltage is HIGH — treat as energized work
Discharge cable after testing — ground for 4x test duration
Dedicated safety watch at each cable end
All workers clear — verbal confirmation chain
Discharge and verify zero volts before approaching

de_energized: Verify zero energy with CAT III/IV rated tester
Ground phase conductors for MV equipment

fall_protection: Do NOT walk on cable tray unless rated and approved
Tool lanyards required — dropped bolt from height is lethal
installation: Support spacing per manufacturer (5-8 ft ladder, 4-6 ft solid)
Don't exceed tray fill per NEC 392.22

requirements: Atmospheric monitoring: O2, LEL, CO, H2S
Attendant at entry at all times
De-energize circuits in space if possible
Hot work permit required within 35 ft of combustibles
Fire watch during and 30 min after
Never use conduit as welding ground path
Ground welding leads directly to workpiece
Shield cable insulation from grinding sparks
